message:
fix(storage): use retry-enabled commit path for archive initialization

ensure_repo_initialized previously used direct commit_paths() for the
initial .gitattributes commit, bypassing the retry logic that protects
all other archive write paths against index.lock contention. If a stale
lock or concurrent git operation existed at init time, archive setup
would fail before the retry mechanism could engage.

Switches to commit_paths_with_retry() for parity with the rest of the
archive write pipeline, making initialization resilient to the same
transient lock contention that other paths already handle gracefully.

Also updates beads tracker with new in-progress issue br-lxht tracking
this fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -530,11 +530,13 @@
 {"id":"br-3ubu.2","title":"Conformance harness hygiene: stop mutating tracked python_reference/_scratch archives","description":"Problem: The conformance fixture generator/harness has a python_reference/_scratch area that contains git archives. These directories are currently tracked in git and can be mutated by runs, leaving a dirty worktree. Even with .gitignore, tracked scratch dirs stay tracked, so any mutation creates noisy diffs and risks committing generated state.\n\nDeliverables:\n- Make conformance generation and test execution hermetic: any writable/scratch archives must be copied to a temp dir (e.g., via tempfile) before mutation. Never write into the committed fixture tree.\n- Add an explicit regression test/guard: running conformance should not modify any files under crates/mcp-agent-mail-conformance/tests/conformance/python_reference (especially _scratch).\n- Optionally (needs explicit permission for deletion): remove committed _scratch artifacts from the repo once safe, relying on .gitignore and regenerated fixtures instead.\n\nDefinition of done:\n- cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-conformance does not dirty the repo.\n- When fixture regeneration is needed, output paths are explicit and default to /tmp, not under the repo.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-08T23:08:27.464449798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T23:21:54.009038249Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T23:21:54.009017661Z","close_reason":"Conformance generator no longer writes scratch/fixtures into repo by default; guard test + docs + CLI flag","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3ubu.2","depends_on_id":"br-3ubu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T23:08:27.464449798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":58,"issue_id":"br-3ubu.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented hermetic conformance fixture generation defaults (no repo writes):

- crates/mcp-agent-mail-conformance/tests/conformance/python_reference/generate_fixtures.py
  - _mk_run_dir() now defaults to OS temp via tempfile.mkdtemp(...) (never under the repo).
  - Supports override via MCP_AGENT_MAIL_CONFORMANCE_SCRATCH_ROOT.
  - Safety guard: refuses scratch roots under tests/conformance/python_reference/ unless MCP_AGENT_MAIL_CONFORMANCE_ALLOW_REPO_SCRATCH=1 is explicitly set.

- crates/mcp-agent-mail-conformance/src/main.rs
  - regen now defaults --output to a timestamped path under std::env::temp_dir()/mcp-agent-mail-conformance/ so running regen does not implicitly dirty tracked fixtures.
  - Added --scratch-root flag to pass MCP_AGENT_MAIL_CONFORMANCE_SCRATCH_ROOT through to the Python generator.
  - Added a regression unit test guarding against returning to repo-local python_reference/_scratch.

- crates/mcp-agent-mail-conformance/tests/conformance/README.md updated with new safe-default behavior + scratch-root usage.

Validation:
- cargo fmt -p mcp-agent-mail-conformance --check
- cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-conformance
- Verified git diff --name-only -- crates/mcp-agent-mail-conformance/tests/conformance/python_reference/_scratch is stable across another conformance test run (no further mutations).
","created_at":"2026-02-08T23:21:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-3ubu.3","title":"Interface mode contract: reconcile ADR/spec language with Config InterfaceModeResolver behavior","description":"Problem: There is mild contract drift between docs/specs and code around the 'interface mode' concept (MCP vs CLI), including: (1) ADR/specs emphasizing binary-level separation and no runtime switching, (2) Config::from_env reading INTERFACE_MODE, and (3) InterfaceModeResolver tests asserting env precedence. Currently interface_mode is mostly diagnostic metadata, but drift here risks future bugs and operator confusion.\n\nDeliverables:\n- Decide the canonical contract for INTERFACE_MODE: test-only knob vs supported override vs remove entirely.\n- Align code + comments + ADR/spec wording to the chosen contract.\n- Add regression tests to ensure the MCP server binary cannot be accidentally turned into a CLI surface (and that denial UX remains deterministic).\n\nDefinition of done:\n- ADR/specs + code behavior match; no misleading comments remain; and the misconfig path is tested.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-08T23:08:35.203875494Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T23:38:46.866485369Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T23:38:46.866461104Z","close_reason":"Aligned interface_mode contract with ADR-001: removed INTERFACE_MODE runtime override; binary stamps mode; tests updated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3ubu.3","depends_on_id":"br-3ubu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T23:08:35.203875494Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":59,"issue_id":"br-3ubu.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Reconciled interface-mode contract to match ADR-001/MIGRATION_GUIDE: mode is binary-level only (no INTERFACE_MODE runtime env var).

Code changes:
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/config.rs
  - Removed INTERFACE_MODE env parsing from Config::from_env().
  - Removed InterfaceModeResolver/ResolvedMode/ModeProvenance (unused + contradicted ADR-001 Invariant 3).
  - Updated InterfaceMode docs/comments to state there is intentionally no INTERFACE_MODE env var.
  - Kept InterfaceMode enum + basic unit tests; removed resolver/env-precedence tests.
  - bootstrap_summary now reports interface_mode source as default (binary stamps the value).

- crates/mcp-agent-mail/src/main.rs
  - Stamps config.interface_mode = InterfaceMode::Mcp directly (binary-level, per ADR-001).

- crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/context.rs
  - Stamps Config::from_env() results with InterfaceMode::Cli in both sync and async CLI contexts.

Validation:
- cargo fmt -p mcp-agent-mail-core -p mcp-agent-mail -p mcp-agent-mail-cli --check
- cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-core --all-targets
- cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-cli --all-targets
- cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail --all-targets
- cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-core
- cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-cli --test perf_security_regressions (includes SEC-4 env override bypass attempt)
","created_at":"2026-02-08T23:38:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-51oi","title":"E2E: console suite output-contract failures for startup banner and runtime panels","description":"Repro on 2026-02-09. test_console reports ~20 failures: missing 'Server Configuration', 'Database Statistics', 'ConsoleCaps', persisted layout/theme lines, TOOL CALL panel, request panel lines, and left-split summary markers. Align runtime output with test/spec or update tests to new canonical contract.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-09T06:14:33.156554081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T10:09:02.488453100Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T10:09:02.488435277Z","close_reason":"Completed: console e2e assertions aligned to current startup/runtime contract; suite passes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["console","e2e","regression","tui"]}

Ensure resource parsing and query path honor active_only=true by returning only unreleased, unexpired reservations.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-09T15:51:26.020186521Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T15:51:31.306242170Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-9jl","title":"CLI: share verify/decrypt parity (signature + age)","description":"## Objective
Implement `share verify` and `share decrypt` with legacy semantics.

## Scope
- `share verify`:
  - Validate SRI hashes and optional Ed25519 signature.
  - `--public-key` uses provided base64 key; else use manifest signature.
- `share decrypt`:
  - `--output` defaults to filename sans `.age` (or `_decrypted`).
  - `--identity` (age key file) is mutually exclusive with `--passphrase`.

## Tests
- Unit tests for signature verification and decrypt flag validation.
- Integration tests decrypting a bundle produced by share export.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store verification results under `tests/artifacts/cli/share_verify/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. share verify/decrypt match legacy flags, defaults, and exit codes.
2. Signature validation and age decrypt succeed on valid inputs and fail loudly on invalid.
3. Mutual‑exclusion flag handling matches legacy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenDune","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:17.420980860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:15:14.917640762Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:15:14.917621787Z","close_reason":"Added CLI integration tests for share verify/decrypt parity; validated exit codes + defaults","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-9jl","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:44.284948962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-9jl","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:23.973796081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-ave","title":"Share: sqlite snapshot + project scope","description":"## Objective
Implement share pipeline Steps 1–2: `create_sqlite_snapshot` and `apply_project_scope` with exact legacy semantics.

## Scope
- **create_sqlite_snapshot**:
  - Resolve destination path; create parent dirs.
  - Error if destination exists (never overwrite).
  - Open source DB; run `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(PASSIVE)` when enabled.
  - Use sqlite backup API to copy source → destination.
- **apply_project_scope**:
  - Load projects (id, slug, human_key); error if none.
  - If identifiers empty → no deletions.
  - Match identifiers case‑insensitive by slug or human_key; error on unknown.
  - Delete order per spec (agent_links, sibling_suggestions, messages + recipients, file_reservations, agents, projects).

## Tests
- Unit tests for identifier matching + error cases.
- Integration tests with temp DB and multiple projects; verify deletions and counts.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Save before/after row counts under `tests/artifacts/share/snapshot/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Snapshot creation refuses overwrite and produces byte‑identical DB backup.
2. Project scoping deletes only disallowed project data in the specified order.
3. Tests are deterministic and isolated to temp DB paths.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:15:05.665286231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:52:39.363640110Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:52:39.363620834Z","close_reason":"Implemented backup snapshot + scope parity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-ave","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:15:11.619634150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-gtg9","title":"Fix flaky test: to_dict_per_table_sorted_by_count_desc_then_name_asc","description":"Test in tracking.rs fails intermittently in full suite but passes individually. Root cause: serde_json feature unification timing - the order_probe check for preserve_order may give inconsistent results depending on compilation order. The probe detects BTreeMap backend but actual per_table map preserves insertion order. Consider removing the probe and always asserting insertion-order (since indexmap feature IS unified in the workspace).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-09T05:32:36.906218063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:36:17.398892938Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:36:17.398862601Z","close_reason":"Fixed: added preserve_order feature to workspace serde_json dep, simplified test to always assert insertion order","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-hk3v","title":"Dev UX: am runner script + /api<->/mcp HTTP path alias","description":"## Problem
Running the local HTTP server currently requires multiple manual exports (bearer token from a separate envfile, HTTP_PATH override, LOG_RICH_ENABLED) and is error-prone. In this environment some clients expect /mcp/ while the server defaults to /api/.

## Goals
- One easy entrypoint: am serve starts the server with sane defaults and rich console enabled by default.
- Auto-load HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN from a dev envfile (default: ~/mcp_agent_mail/.env) if not already set.
- Support BOTH /api/ and /mcp/ for the MCP HTTP endpoint (alias each other so either client works).
- Keep backward compatibility for existing E2E scripts that hardcode /api/.

## Implementation
1) Add scripts/am shell wrapper:
- am serve (default) runs: cargo run -p mcp-agent-mail -- serve --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8765
- Defaults: HTTP_PATH=/mcp/ and LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true
- Options: --path mcp|api, --env-file <path>, --host, --port, --no-auth
- If HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN is unset, attempt to read it from env file (strip quotes).

2) Server: accept /api and /mcp as synonyms for the MCP base path:
- If configured base is /api, also accept requests under /mcp by rewriting the request path to the configured base before passing to HttpRequestHandler (configured with base_path=config.http_path).
- If configured base is /mcp, also accept /api similarly.
- Do not change behavior for arbitrary nested bases (for example /api/mcp).

3) Docs: update README.md with scripts/am usage and the /api + /mcp alias note.

## Tests
- Unit tests for the alias path logic (path_allowed + canonicalization).

## Acceptance Criteria
- scripts/am serve works without manual exports in a typical dev setup (token file present).
- With default config (HTTP_PATH unset), both POST /api/ and POST /mcp/ reach the MCP handler.
- Full quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test.
